ud6.2:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at the Eastern Monastery, the palace of Migāra’s mother.
ud6.2:1.3:
And on that occasion the Blessed One, having emerged from his seclusion in the late afternoon,
was sitting outside the doorway of the porch.
ud6.2:1.4: Then King Pasenadi Kosala went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
ud6.2:2.1:
Now on that occasion seven coiled-hair ascetics, seven Jain ascetics,
seven cloth-less ascetics, seven one-cloth ascetics, & seven wanderers–
their nails, armpit-hair, & body-hair grown long,
carrying containers on poles [over their shoulders]–walked past, not far from the Blessed One.
ud6.2:3.1:
King Pasenadi Kosala saw the seven coiled-hair ascetics, seven Jain ascetics,
seven cloth-less ascetics, seven one-cloth ascetics, & seven wanderers–
their nails, armpit-hair, & body-hair grown long,
carrying containers on poles [over their shoulders]–walking past, not far from the Blessed One.
ud6.2:3.2:
On seeing them, he got up from his seat, arranged his upper robe over one shoulder,
knelt down with his right knee on the ground, paid homage to the seven coiled-hair ascetics,
seven Jain ascetics, seven cloth-less ascetics, seven one-cloth ascetics,
& seven wanderers with his hands palm-to-palm in front his heart, and announced his name three times:
ud6.2:3.3: “I am the king, venerable sirs, Pasenadi Kosala.
ud6.2:3.4: I am the king, venerable sirs, Pasenadi Kosala.
ud6.2:3.5: I am the king, venerable sirs, Pasenadi Kosala.”
ud6.2:4.1:
Then not long after the seven coiled-hair ascetics, seven Jain ascetics,
seven cloth-less ascetics, seven one-cloth ascetics,
& seven wanderers had passed, King Pasenadi Kosala went to the Blessed One and,
on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side.
As he was sitting there he said to the Blessed One,
ud6.2:4.2: “Of those in the world who are arahants or on the path to arahantship, are these among them?”
ud6.2:7.2: Astounding!–how well that was put by the Blessed One!
ud6.2:7.5: “‘It’s through discussion that a person’s discernment may be known,
ud6.2:8.1: “These men, lord, are my spies, my scouts, returning after going out through the countryside.
ud6.2:8.2: They go out first, and then I go.
ud6.2:8.3:
Now, when they have scrubbed off the dirt & mud, are well-bathed & well-perfumed, have trimmed their hair and beards,
and have put on white clothes, they will go about endowed and provided with the five strings of sensuality.”
ud6.2:9.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud6.2:10.1: One should not make an effort everywhere,
ud6.2:10.3: should not live dependent on another,
ud6.2:10.4: should not go about as a trader in the Dhamma.
